storage_policy.copyright_boundary: Store source identity, locator, short observations, and derived design signals. Do not store copied prose, screenshots, frames, audio, video, brand marks, layouts, source assets, or full transcripts.

anchors.locator: article-level hierarchy principle
anchors.observation: A presentation hierarchy lesson should become a slide contract: one primary claim, one dominant proof object, and visibly subordinate support.
anchors.extracted_design_signal: Reject slides where all panels have equal visual weight or the main claim competes with multiple same-size proof objects.

anchors.locator: visual examples observed at page level
anchors.observation: Image references are useful only as composition analysis: focal scale, whitespace, contrast, and reading order.
anchors.extracted_design_signal: Generate original editable PPT compositions that express focal hierarchy without copying source layouts or screenshots.

anchors.locator: white-space guidance summarized as rule
anchors.observation: Whitespace guidance should become a measurable density budget, not a generic instruction to make the slide cleaner.
anchors.extracted_design_signal: Every main-story slide must choose max words, max panels, max proof objects, and an explicit route for excess proof.

anchors.locator: page-level visual reference analysis
anchors.observation: Useful whitespace is active composition space around the focal object, not empty decoration.
anchors.extracted_design_signal: The generator must reserve a dominant field on cover, climax, and close slides rather than filling the canvas with equal cards.

anchors.locator: 00:00-00:45 visual opening beat
anchors.observation: Opening video rhythm can be translated into one wide visual field before explanatory panels appear.
anchors.extracted_design_signal: The cover slide must begin with a dominant visual object or field and fewer panels than later proof slides.

anchors.locator: 00:00-00:45 speech cadence observation
anchors.observation: Audio pacing implies density control: when narration leaves attention space, the slide should not overfill that beat with labels.
anchors.extracted_design_signal: Reduce visible words on orientation beats and avoid dense labels before the mechanism has been introduced.

anchors.locator: short paraphrased claim anchor only
anchors.observation: Transcript analysis should compress spoken explanation into a slide headline and speaker-note support, not copy phrasing.
anchors.extracted_design_signal: Convert long explanation into one native headline, one short support line, and optional speaker notes; do not place full transcript text on slide.

qa_gates: spoken content is paraphrased into design signals

anchors.locator: product behavior described at page level
anchors.observation: A presentation product reference is useful when its interaction model becomes generator controls, not when its UI is copied.
anchors.extracted_design_signal: Expose slide-generation controls as inspectable state: selected memory ids, density budget, asset policy, and release gate.

anchors.locator: public product visuals observed as high-level reference
anchors.observation: Product screenshots should not be copied; they can inspire original native surfaces that make generation state legible.
anchors.extracted_design_signal: Use original UI-like panels only when they explain product behavior, not as decorative dashboard filler.

anchors.locator: case-study description summarized as event design need
anchors.observation: Large-scale keynote references should be used to enforce a visible climax, not to borrow event graphics.
anchors.extracted_design_signal: Reserve one slide for the largest supported outcome object and ensure it breaks from setup/proof density.

anchors.locator: audience journey inferred from case structure
anchors.observation: A public keynote close should hand the audience to a decision, next action, or proof checkpoint.
anchors.extracted_design_signal: The close slide must include one decision handoff and one release gate, not an appendix-like status grid.

anchors.locator: public page learning outcome about visually enhancing important data and removing the rest
anchors.observation: A visual storytelling lesson should force the generator to separate the important data from surrounding explanation.
anchors.extracted_design_signal: Generate a before/after delta where the after state enlarges one data object and routes nonessential explanation to notes or trace detail.

anchors.locator: public page description of motivating support for an idea
anchors.observation: Presentation storytelling is not just clearer layout; it should end with audience belief or action.
anchors.extracted_design_signal: The close slide must preserve one decision handoff and one reason to act, with no appendix-like status grid.

anchors.locator: 45-minute webinar metadata on public page
anchors.observation: A long-form video/webinar implies repeated attention resets; a six-slide demo needs planned rhythm changes rather than six equal panels.
anchors.extracted_design_signal: Use a rhythm ladder: orientation slide under 30 visible words, proof slide under 55 words, climax slide with the largest proof object.

anchors.locator: webinar audio channel acknowledged as source modality; no audio saved
anchors.observation: Audio teaching moments should be translated into attention space, not copied speech.
anchors.extracted_design_signal: When a slide represents a narrated explanation, compress the visible slide to headline plus proof object and route extra explanation to speaker notes.

anchors.locator: public course overview statement about directing audience attention
anchors.observation: Slide design training should become an attention-directing requirement, not a decorative style prompt.
anchors.extracted_design_signal: Every generated slide must name its focal object and prove that no other same-scale object competes with it.

anchors.locator: public course module list mentioning PowerPoint UI/tool operations
anchors.observation: Tooling lessons should become editable PPT implementation requirements.
anchors.extracted_design_signal: Prefer native PowerPoint shapes, alignment guides, selection-pane-friendly groups, editable text, and transitions recorded as metadata instead of flattened slide images.

anchors.locator: public page image references observed only as high-level composition cues
anchors.observation: Course imagery can inform polish cues but cannot become copied layout, screenshot, or asset memory.
anchors.extracted_design_signal: Generate original mini-previews with restrained spacing, strong focal scale, and native labels; do not copy course visuals.

anchors.locator: public course metadata: on-demand video modules
anchors.observation: Video-module training implies stepwise transformation that should be visible in the generated deck.
anchors.extracted_design_signal: At least one slide must show an original before/after mini-preview pair where a report-like input becomes a designed output.

qa_gates: only public course metadata is used

anchors.locator: public tutorial description naming proximity, repetition, alignment, and contrast
anchors.observation: General design principles become useful only when mapped to slide-level QA checks.
anchors.extracted_design_signal: Each generated target must include proximity grouping, repeated style tokens, alignment constraints, and one contrast mechanism.

anchors.locator: 7:19 video tutorial metadata; no frames saved
anchors.observation: A tutorial that shows principles in action should produce a visible transformation target rather than a text summary.
anchors.extracted_design_signal: Generate a native before/after slide pair that demonstrates alignment and contrast changes without copying tutorial frames.

anchors.locator: video audio channel acknowledged as source modality; no audio saved
anchors.observation: Stepwise instruction should become sequential slide reveal logic, not dense static explanation.
anchors.extracted_design_signal: Use staged native slide groups with a recorded reveal order and no more than three visible stages at once.

anchors.locator: public page lists written and time-code transcript links; transcript text not stored
anchors.observation: Transcript availability is useful for locator precision, but the generated slide should paraphrase the lesson into native labels.
anchors.extracted_design_signal: Use transcript only as locator metadata; convert principle explanation into short editable labels and speaker notes, not copied transcript text.

anchors.locator: public license area noting CC BY-NC for video content
anchors.observation: The license makes the public-packaging boundary explicit; this case pack should still avoid storing or redistributing the source media.
anchors.extracted_design_signal: Use internal-only release labels on any slide target using this source until provenance and human approval explicitly clear public packaging.

qa_gates: transcript used only as locator metadata

cross_modal_design_tasks.task: Convert tutorial and image-reference observations into an original before/after slide target.
cross_modal_design_tasks.required_generator_behavior: The full-skill arm must show at least one visible transformation from flat report layout to stronger visual hierarchy.

cross_modal_design_tasks.task: Translate pacing observations into slide density and rhythm budgets.
cross_modal_design_tasks.required_generator_behavior: Orientation beats use fewer visible words and larger visual fields; proof beats may add detail only after the focal object is established.

cross_modal_design_tasks.task: Compress spoken or written tutorial explanation into native editable headline/support text.
cross_modal_design_tasks.required_generator_behavior: Long explanations route to speaker notes or trace notes instead of becoming tiny slide text.

cross_modal_design_tasks.task: Turn product and workflow observations into inspectable generation controls.
cross_modal_design_tasks.required_generator_behavior: Slides expose selected memory, density budget, and release gate only when those controls explain product behavior.

cross_modal_design_tasks.task: Convert tutorial observations into original native before/after slide mini-previews.
cross_modal_design_tasks.required_generator_behavior: The full-skill arm must show at least one editable mini-preview pair that proves a design transformation.

cross_modal_design_tasks.task: Map proximity, repetition, alignment, and contrast into layout QA probes.
cross_modal_design_tasks.required_generator_behavior: Generated slides must record grouping, token repetition, alignment, and contrast checks in the trace.

qa_gates: all required modalities appear in at least one record
every record references a source in sources.json
every anchor has a locator, observation, extracted design signal, and allowed use
raw audio, video, screenshots, source layouts, full transcripts, and copied prose are not stored
cross-modal tasks produce generator behavior, not just documentation
license and copyright boundaries are reviewed before any public packaging
